# Key Levenchuk LJ posts — captured 2026-05-17

> Captured via Claude Code WebFetch tool. Verbatim quotes preserved; structural
> extraction by WebFetch summarizer. Provenance per post.

## Post 1 — «Альфы и артефакты/«рабочие продукты»» (2024-04, LJ id 1718836)

**URL:** https://ailev.livejournal.com/1718836.html
**Retrieved:** 2026-05-17

### Verbatim definitions

- **Alpha definition:** «важный предмет метода, меняющий своё состояние в ходе проекта»
- **Alpha vs Work product:** «продукт/изделие/система и «рабочий продукт»/артефакт всё-таки разные понятия» (products are typically physical; work products may be descriptions despite documentary physicality)
- **Work products:** «всё-таки могут указывать на описания» (work products can reference descriptions rather than physical things)

### State graph encoding

- Past-participle verbal forms denoting completed method operations
- Example for "nail-driver" (инструмент):
  - «потребность определена» (need defined)
  - «быть выбранным» (selected as option)
  - «быть закупленным» (procured)
  - «быть проверенным в работе» (tested in use)
- **«полноценный граф состояний, с циклами»** — complete state graph with cycles, not simple linear progressions

### Current ShSM kernel (4 primary alphas)

1. System embodiment (воплощение системы)
2. System description (описание системы)
3. Way of working/function (функция/метод работ)
4. System operations (работы системы)

Consistent across projects while specific work products vary by organizational context.

## Post 2 — «Планы FPF» (2026-02, LJ id 1788706)

**URL:** https://ailev.livejournal.com/1788706.html
**Retrieved:** 2026-05-17

### Immediate priorities Feb 2026

- «убрать противоречия с онтологией холонов, методов, работ, ролей» — resolve ontological contradictions across holons, methods, works, roles
- Clean definitions in patterns B.1.5, B.1.6, A.14, A.2
- Establish Method as design-time capability, Work as run-time enactment

### Language Precision Cluster

«управление сжатием-разжатием в языке» (compression-decompression management in language) via pattern A.6.P, involving routing of context/boundary statements and lexical precision in signatures/relations/interfaces.

### Subsequent phases

- TGA (Transduction Graph Architecture) for engineering processes
- Problem-solving factory expansion (patterns like F.20 "ProblemCard@Context")
- Holonic conflict ontology
- Architecture characteristics (Q-bundle framework)
- SoTA-packs (Part G) and domain extensions

### AI tooling used by Левенчук

- GPT-5.2 Pro in extended thinking mode analyzing «архитектуру управления точностью и сжатостью»

### Timeline

«Планы нужны, чтобы их выкидывать» — plans exist to be discarded; flexible iteration.

### IWE / U.Episteme / Intellect-Stack

**Not explicitly mentioned in this post.** FPF dev is internally architecture-focused; IWE positioning лежит в Цэрэновском материале, не в Левенчуковском FPF spec.

## Post 3 — «10-я конференция МИМ — программа» (2026-04, LJ id 1798285)

**URL:** https://ailev.livejournal.com/1798285.html
**Retrieved:** 2026-05-17

### Event basics

- **Dates:** April 18-19, 2026
- **Location:** МИМ office Moscow (Taganka) + online
- **Cost:** Free
- **Format:** 20 talks across 6 sections

### Day 1 (April 18)

**Section 1 — Workshop Development:**
- Tseren Tserenov: "МИМ in 2025-2026 and future prospects"
- Anatoly Levenchuk: "Development for the developed at МИМ"

**Section 2 — First/Second/Third Principles Frameworks & AI Agents:**
- Levenchuk: "First Principles Framework development plans"
- **Tserenov: "AI in МИМ: IWE and personal guidance"** ← key IWE talk
- Ilshat Gabdulin: "Mastering seminars using AI-agents"

**Section 3 — Research Development:**
- Viktor Agroskin: Ontological examination of economics/finance
- Anna Lubchenko: "Operational management ontology research"

**Section 4 — Personal Development:**
- Pyotr Leontiev: «System fitness work in 2025-2026»
- Danil Shipilov: Personal transformation experience at МИМ
- **Stanislav Sergeev: "IWE as personal assistant for agricultural consultants"** ← IWE applied case

### Day 2 (April 19)

**Section 5 — Professional Development:**
- Denis Asfandiyarov: Breakthrough technology organization in oil/gas
- Mikhail Bukharin, Vladimir Bodrov, Ilya Burdin: System thinking for tech entrepreneurs
- **Timur Batyrshin: "Using FPF for service request ontology"** ← FPF applied case
- Yulia Chaikovskaya: Infrastructure for scaling
- Stanislav Nedbailov: "Influence without authority"
- Ivan Podobed: "Engineering methods as critical competency"

**Section 6 — Children & Community Development:**
- Liya Sultanova: "Transdisciplinary intellect-stack in preschool education"
- Asfandiyarov: "Zero and first principles guidance for children"
- Alena Chaikovskaya: "Scaling intellectual expertise"

### Notable

- **IWE is a featured topic** — Tserenov leads dedicated talk + Sergeev shows applied case (agricultural consulting).
- **FPF used by external practitioners** — Batyrshin "Using FPF for service request ontology".
- **«Развитие для развитых»** (Development for the developed) — Левенчуковский talk title; advanced practitioner track.
- **SPF/TPF** — Second/Third Principles Frameworks — section title indicates these are coming.

## Post 4 — «Осенняя резидентура» (2025-09, LJ id 1777145)

**URL:** https://ailev.livejournal.com/1777145.html
**Retrieved:** 2026-05-17

### Three program tracks (Autumn 2025 cohort)

**1. Rational Work** (R0; no prerequisites)
- Kickoff: 2025-10-04
- Sessions: Tuesdays 18:00, Saturdays 11:30
- First session: 2025-10-07; final: 2025-12-13
- Weekly commitment: **не менее 10-15 часов**

**2. Systems Thinking & Methodology** (R1; requires R0)
- Kickoff: 2026-02-03
- Sessions: Tuesdays 18:00-20:00
- First session: 2026-02-10; final: 2026-06-09
- **19 total sessions**

**3. Systems Management & Engineering** (R2; requires R0+R1)
- Kickoff: 2025-10-08
- Sessions: Wednesdays 18:00-20:00
- First session: 2025-10-15; final: 2026-02-18
- **19 total sessions**

### Format

- Mentor-led **«разборы»** (review sessions) of participants' actual work projects.
- NOT traditional lectures.
- Session duration adjusts based on cohort size «to allow sufficient discussion time for each participant's projects».
- **No mention of AI in this announcement** (but IWE is the cross-cutting layer per other posts).

## Post 5 — «Методология в интеллект-стеке» (2024, LJ id 1725757)

**URL:** https://ailev.livejournal.com/1725757.html
**Retrieved:** 2026-05-17

### Position of methodology layer

Methodology enables agents to «describe ways of work of creators/agents in graphs of creating successful systems». Modern methodology «accounts for teams of agents and collectives (teams of teams)».

### Core challenge addressed

Agents with «very limited arsenal of elementary operations» must «map enormous multidimensional space of incoming information into action space of small dimensionality».

### Three approaches to method selection

**1. Applied Methodology (Domain Knowledge)** — «in concrete situation with concrete domain objects—take theories/knowledge/explanations/algorithms of domain, and act»

**2. Fundamental Methodology (System Thinking)** — when domain is unknown: «we select important objects from general cultural, fundamental knowledge…determine what systems exist, what methods they use, what roles they play»

**3. Distributed Representations (AI/Neural Networks)** — «neuronal networks can perform pattern detection both of method subjects and patterns of effective actions with them»

### Methodology connects to:

- **Semantics** — theory of representations (local + distributed)
- **Ontology** — abstraction levels + important objects
- **Rationality** — «knowledge in decision-making theories» for method-alternative selection
- **Ethics + Rhetoric** — constraining choices + persuading other agents

### Important note

«For collective discussion of methods and their evolution, we still require local representations» despite distributed systems' power.

---

## Post 6 — Psybertron English review (Ian Glendinning, 2022)

**URL:** https://www.psybertron.org/archives/15807
**Retrieved:** 2026-05-17

### Distinctions established by Левенчук

- **Systems Project Engineering** (execution-focused)
- **Systems Engineering Thinking** (intermediate layer)
- **Systems Thinking** (strategic architecting and understanding)

«distinct areas (layers) of consideration», not substitutes.

### Devil/angel metaphor

«The devil in the details and an angel in the abstractions» — appropriate abstraction levels matter contextually. Strategic architecting requires understanding which details to ignore; execution demands practical detail knowledge.

### «Definition as Coffin for a Dead Think»

Premature formal definitions constrain ongoing thinking. «In systems thinking, there is always thinking to be done.» Meanings emerge statistically through usage across contexts.

### Verbatim Левенчук (via Glendinning translation)

> «The meanings of terms (and any other words, even if they are not called 'terms') are determined statistically, not precisely» through examining «diagrams, texts, and sets of expanded statements containing the term of interest».

### Glendinning's framing of Левенчук's third-generation

Connects to cybernetics' original human-centered focus (1946 Macy Conference) — Левенчук recovers what was lost when early practitioners forgot the governance intentions of «kybernetes».

## Post 7 — Tseren Tserenov community profile (systemsworld.club)

**URL:** https://systemsworld.club/t/czeren-czerenov-strateg-sistemnogo-myshleniya-i-sozdatel-uspeshnyh-sistem/22932
**Retrieved:** 2026-05-17

### Identity

- Co-founder ШСМ; mehmat MGU (mathematics); provincial Soviet-era town origin.

### Core philosophy

Success framed as **«a new level of problems»** rather than problem-free existence. Personal development as engineering project. Happiness/success = **«man-made products»** systematically constructable.

### Methodology emphasis

- Rapid learning + holistic perspective
- Incremental goal-setting + scaling initiatives
- Progressive expansion of personal agency + caliber: individual → organizational → societal levels

### Mission statement

To help hardworking individuals «live interestingly, happily, and be successful» by systemic thinking, continuous learning, bold world-transformation.

### Subculture being built

**«Subculture of systems self-development»** — people learn systematic thinking to improve environment through creating successful systems.

### IWE involvement

Not explicitly detailed in this profile, but cross-referenced per LJ post 3 (Tserenov is the lead for IWE talk at MIM 10th conf).

## Post 9 — Intellect-stack listing (system-school.ru/stack)

**URL:** https://system-school.ru/stack
**Retrieved:** 2026-05-17

### 16 transdisciplines (current 2026 version)

1. Engineering (Инженерия)
2. Rhetoric (Риторика)
3. Aesthetics (Эстетика)
4. Research (Исследования)
5. Algorithmics (Алгоритмика)
6. Theory of Concepts (Теория понятий)
7. Mathematics (Математика)
8. Collectedness (Собранность)
9. Methodology (Методология)
10. Ethics (Этика)
11. Rationality (Рациональность)
12. Ontology (Онтология)
13. Physics (Физика)
14. Semantics (Семантика)
15. Conceptualization (Понятизация)
16. Logic (Логика)

### Framing

Page presents these as «tracing connections between scientific-technical and humanities knowledge», positioning as alternative to STEM framework.

### Resolution of «17 vs 16» ambiguity

Inventory §1 Category 09.x noted ambiguity. Verified as of 2026-05-17: **current count = 16**. 17 was 2021 version (per R-A §4.1, superseded). Truth from current canonical page wins.
